---
title: "Research | Ayel L. R. Batac"
title-block: false
canonical-url: https://ayelbatac.github.io/research
description: "Explore Ayel L. R. Batac’s research in allergy and clinical immunology, infectious diseases, and global health."
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    anchor-links: false
---

# RESEARCH

## RESEARCH SUMMARY

My research centres on the social epidemiology of sexually transmitted infections (STIs), with a focus on marginalised and underserved populations, particularly men who have sex with men (MSM) and sex worker communities. I am interested in STI prevention and control, and in how structural and behavioural factors contribute to unequal access to testing and other STI services.

I also examine infectious disease prevention and response in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) and other low-resource settings. My current work evaluates whether a pay-it-forward approach, through which individuals receive gonorrhoea and chlamydia testing as a gift and may contribute towards testing for someone else, can increase testing uptake among MSM in China.

My research is grounded primarily in mixed methods and draws on epidemiology and the social and behavioural sciences. I am also interested in community-based and participatory research, including crowdsourcing. Crowdsourcing is a problem-solving approach that draws on collective intelligence by inviting a broad group of people to propose and share solutions to a defined health problem. My work in knowledge translation and knowledge mobilisation examines how research findings and community-generated solutions are used in public health decision-making.

I maintain broader interests in respiratory infections and tropical diseases that contribute substantially to the burden of disease in LMICs. I am also developing interests in health economics and infectious disease modelling. My work in health economics focuses on the economic evaluation of HIV prevention using pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP) and STI prevention using doxycycline post-exposure prophylaxis (doxy-PEP). In infectious disease modelling, I am particularly interested in participatory mathematical modelling.

Alongside my work in infectious diseases, I have a longstanding interest in allergy and clinical immunology, particularly anaphylaxis and allergen immunotherapy.

$~$

<h2 style="color: #1B153E; font-weight: 600; border-bottom: none; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">CURRENT RESEARCH</h2>

Learn more about each research theme by clicking on each tile.

<div style="margin-bottom: 2rem;"></div>

<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(250px, 1fr)); gap: 1.5rem; margin-bottom: 2rem;">

  <!-- STI -->
  <a href="sti.qmd" style="text-decoration: none;">
    <img src="sti.png" alt="STIs" style="width: 100%; border-radius: 10px; transition: transform 0.3s ease, box-shadow 0.3s ease;"
         onmouseover="this.style.transform='scale(1.05)'; this.style.boxShadow='0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)';"
         onmouseout="this.style.transform='scale(1)'; this.style.boxShadow='none';">
  </a>

  <!-- Diagnostics -->
  <a href="diagnostics.qmd" style="text-decoration: none;">
    <img src="diagnostics1.png" alt="Diagnostics" style="width: 100%; border-radius: 10px; transition: transform 0.3s ease, box-shadow 0.3s ease;"
         onmouseover="this.style.transform='scale(1.05)'; this.style.boxShadow='0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)';"
         onmouseout="this.style.transform='scale(1)'; this.style.boxShadow='none';">
  </a>

  <!-- Tuberculosis -->
  <a href="tuberculosis.qmd" style="text-decoration: none;">
    <img src="tb.png" alt="Tuberculosis" style="width: 100%; border-radius: 10px; transition: transform 0.3s ease, box-shadow 0.3s ease;"
         onmouseover="this.style.transform='scale(1.05)'; this.style.boxShadow='0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)';"
         onmouseout="this.style.transform='scale(1)'; this.style.boxShadow='none';">
  </a>

  <!-- Mpox -->
  <a href="mpox.qmd" style="text-decoration: none;">
    <img src="mpox.png" alt="Mpox" style="width: 100%; border-radius: 10px; transition: transform 0.3s ease, box-shadow 0.3s ease;"
         onmouseover="this.style.transform='scale(1.05)'; this.style.boxShadow='0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)';"
         onmouseout="this.style.transform='scale(1)'; this.style.boxShadow='none';">
  </a>

  <!-- Anaphylaxis -->
  <a href="anaphylaxis.qmd" style="text-decoration: none;">
    <img src="anaphylaxis.png" alt="Anaphylaxis" style="width: 100%; border-radius: 10px; transition: transform 0.3s ease, box-shadow 0.3s ease;"
         onmouseover="this.style.transform='scale(1.05)'; this.style.boxShadow='0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)';"
         onmouseout="this.style.transform='scale(1)'; this.style.boxShadow='none';">
  </a>

</div>

$~$

## RESEARCH INTERESTS

- Social epidemiology of sexually transmitted infections (STIs)  
- Infectious disease prevention and control in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) and low-resource settings  
- Respiratory tract infections (COVID-19, tuberculosis)  
- Community-based and participatory research    
- Allergy (anaphylaxis, asthma, atopic dermatitis, drug allergy, food allergy)  
- Allergen immunotherapy (subcutaneous immunotherapy, sublingual immunotherapy)  
- Dermatoepidemiology  
- Behavioural interventions for health service uptake  
- Diagnostics   
- Evidence synthesis in allergy and infectious diseases  
- Implementation science  
- Mathematical modelling (infectious diseases)    
- Health economic evaluation (allergy and infectious disease interventions)

## RESEARCH AREAS

- Allergy and Clinical Immunology   
- Infectious Diseases  
- Sexually Transmitted Infections (STIs)  
- HIV/AIDS  
- Dermatology  
- Pediatrics  
- Public Health  
- Global Health  
- Epidemiology (Communicable and Non-Communicable Diseases)  
- Health Systems Research  
- Health Policy   
- Health Economics  
- Implementation Science  

## KEYWORDS

Allergy, anaphylaxis, atopic dermatitis, drug allergy, food allergy, infectious diseases, sexually transmitted infections, STI, gonorrhea, chlamydia, syphilis, HIV/AIDS, coronavirus disease 2019, COVID-19, tuberculosis, TB, diagnostics, sexual health, stigma, public health, global health, mixed methods, implementation science, health systems research, health policy, evidence synthesis, low- and middle-income countries, LMIC, health equity

$~$

```{=html}
<!-- Research Platforms Section -->
<div style="
  width: 100%;
padding: 0 0 1.5rem 0;
  background-color: #ffffff;
">

  <h2 style="
    font-size: 1.05rem;
    font-weight: 600;
    color: #1B153E;
    text-align: left;
    margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
    line-height: 1.4;
    border: none;
  ">
    For a complete list of my publications, please visit the following research platforms:
  </h2>

  <div style="
    display: flex;
    justify-content: flex-start;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 0.75rem;
    flex-wrap: nowrap;
    overflow-x: auto;
  ">

    <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=pUPl3Q8AAAAJ&hl=en" target="_blank"
       class="explore-button"
       style="flex: 0 0 auto; padding: 0.4rem 1rem !important; font-size: 0.9rem !important; width: auto !important; min-width: unset !important; white-space: nowrap !important; text-align: center;">
       Google Scholar
    </a>

    <a href="https://www.webofscience.com/wos/author/record/KUF-3735-2024" target="_blank"
       class="explore-button"
       style="flex: 0 0 auto; padding: 0.4rem 1rem !important; font-size: 0.9rem !important; width: auto !important; min-width: unset !important; white-space: nowrap !important; text-align: center;">
       Web of Science
    </a>

    <a href="https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/?term=Batac+ALR%5BAuthor%5D&sort=date" target="_blank"
       class="explore-button"
       style="flex: 0 0 auto; padding: 0.4rem 1rem !important; font-size: 0.9rem !important; width: auto !important; min-width: unset !important; white-space: nowrap !important; text-align: center;">
       PubMed
    </a>

    <a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-5712-5483" target="_blank"
       class="explore-button"
       style="flex: 0 0 auto; padding: 0.4rem 1rem !important; font-size: 0.9rem !important; width: auto !important; min-width: unset !important; white-space: nowrap !important; text-align: center;">
       ORCiD
    </a>

    <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Ayel-Luis-Batac" target="_blank"
       class="explore-button"
       style="flex: 0 0 auto; padding: 0.4rem 1rem !important; font-size: 0.9rem !important; width: auto !important; min-width: unset !important; white-space: nowrap !important; text-align: center;">
       ResearchGate
    </a>

  </div>
</div>

```

$~$
